B1:
Beginning Japanese Dialog Translations 
 
 
 
第１課の３ 
 
Male agent:  
Do you understand Japanese?  
Kiara:  
 
Yes, I understand. 
Female agent: 
That’s great.  Can you introduce yourself? 
Kiara:  
 
How do you do? I am Kiara. Pleased to meet you. 
 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B10:
Beginning Japanese Dialog Translations 
 
 
 
第３課の２ 
 
Kiara:  
Good morning, Teacher. 
(Teacher) Mr. Yamamoto: 
Morning. 
Kiara:  
Please tell me about today’s schedule. 
(Teacher) Mr. Yamamoto: 
Yes. School is from 8 o’clock.  Today, (the 
classes are) social studies, Japanese language, P.E., art, 
music, and English.  Do you have any questions? 
Kiara: 
Yes, I do.  Tomorrow’s schedule and today’s 
schedule are different, aren’t they?  Is the schedule 
different every day? 
(Teacher) Mr. Yamamoto: 
Oh, you mean the daily class schedule?  Yes, 
every day is different. 
 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B11:
Beginning Japanese Dialog Translations 
 
 
 
第３課の３ 
Jun: The computer lab is here.  The gym is way over there. 
Kiara: Oh, I see.  Where is the library? 
Jun: It is way over there. 
Kiara: This high school is really big, isn’t it?  What is next period? 
Jun: Umm.  Next is 4th period. 4th period is math.  
Kiara: What is 5th period? 
Jun: 5th period is English.  6th period is art.  After class, there are clubs.  My 
club is the judo club. 
Kiara: Really?  I’m a 3rd degree brown belt. 
Jun: What?!  3rd degree?  That’s incredible. 
 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B12:
Beginning Japanese Dialog Translations 
 
 
 
第３課の４ 
 
Kiara: 
Teacher, excuse me. It’s a bit hot. Is it all right to 
open the window? 
(Teacher) Mr. Yamamoto: 
Yes, that’s fine.  Please open that window and 
that window over there. Everyone, today, there is no 
homework.  But, there is a quiz. Well then, please take 
out your textbook and a piece of paper.  Also get out a 
pencil. 
Kiara:  
Teacher, excuse me.  Is it all right to write with a pen? 
(Teacher) Mr. Yamamoto: 
No, a pen is no good.  Please write with a pencil.  
Everyone, do you have pencils? 
Everyone: Yes, we do. 
(Teacher) Mr. Yamamoto: 
Good. Well, let’s begin. 
 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B13:
Beginning Japanese Dialog Translations 
 
 
 
第３課の５ 
Jun: Hello, Ben. 
Ben: Hello. It’s hot, isn’t it? Kiara, how is Japanese school? 
Kiara: It’s fun. But it’s hot and humid, isn’t it? We don’t have air 
conditioning at our school. How is the middle school? 
Ben: It’s cool.  My middle school does have air conditioning, you know.  
(a very strong wind comes out of nowhere) 
Ben: Wow!  That’s some wind, isn’t it? 
Jun: Let’s go to the torii gate of that Shinto shrine over there! 
Kiara: What! Where are we? 
 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B14:
第４課の１ 
 
Ben:  
 
Who are you? 
Jun:  
 
What is that? 
Kiara: 
 
Where did that wind come from? 
Tomo: 
Everyone, wait a minute please.  My name is 
Tomo.  That wind is from that torii gate.  That 
torii gate is a time gate. 
Jun:  
 
A time gate? 
Ben and Kiara: What is that? 
Tomo: 
 
Be a little quiet please. 
 
 
 
Where are you guys from? 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B15:
第9 課の５ 
 
Ben: 
Kiara, you are always wearing that bracelet aren’t you.  
It’s cute. 
Kiara: 
Oh, this?  My sister gave this to me.  It was a birthday 
present. 
Ben: 
Really?  I received this shirt for my birthday from my older 
brother. 
Kiara: 
My younger sister’s birthday is the 4
th of July.  I want to 
give a cool present to her. 
Jun: 
What will you give? 
Kiara: 
I’ll give a kimono and lots of Japanese sweets and her kanji 
name. 
 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B2:
Beginning Japanese Dialog Translations 
 
 
 
第1 課の４ 
 
[sign] Welcome Kiara! 
Kiara: 
How do you do? I am Kiara.  Please treat me favorably.  Are you 
Jun? 
Jun: 
Yes, that’s right. I am Jun. Welcome! Please treat us favorably.  
This person is my mother.  This person is my father.  
Mayumi: 
How do you do? 
Tarou: 
I am Jun’s father.I am very pleased to meet you. 
 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B3:
Beginning Japanese Dialog Translations 
 
 
 
第１課の５ 
 
Aiko:  
Jun, I’m home. Where is Kiara? 
Jun:  
Welcome home. Kiara is in her room. 
Aiko:  
Good evening. 
Kiara:  
Good evening. 
Aiko:  
How do you do? I am Aiko. 
Kiara:  
How do you do? I am Kiara. I am pleased to meet you. 
Mayumi: 
This is sushi. These are chopsticks. Here, go ahead, please. 
Kiara:  
Which is the soy sauce? 
Mayumi: 
That over there. That is wasabi. 
Kiara:  
Is that the egg? 
Jun:  
Yes, that’s right. 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B4:
第２課の１ 
Jun:  
What is that? 
Kiara:  
This is my bag.  Inside, there is a photo.  This is my family. 
My family is six people.  This is my father and mother.  I 
have two younger sisters.  These two are my younger 
sisters.  This is my older brother.  My dog’s name is Koma. 
 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B5:
第２課の２ 
 
Jun:  
 
This is my friend’s house. 
 
 
 
[knock knock] Excuse me. 
Ben’s mother: 
Yes, yes.  Jun, hello. 
Jun:  
 
Hello.  This is Ben’s mother. 
Ben’s mother: 
I am Cathy. 
Kiara: 
Hello.  I’m Kiara.  I’m staying with Jun’s family.  Is 
it all right if we speak in Japanese? 
Ben’s Mother: 
Yes, that’s fine!  It’s Japan, after all.  Do your best!  
Please, step up and come in (the house). 
Jun: 
This is Ben’s older brother.  He’s Jack.  These are 
his younger sisters.  Kara and Sarah. 
Kiara: 
How do you do?  
 
 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B6:
第２課の3 
 
Kiara: 
In this bag there are souvenirs for everyone.  Please (have 
them). 
Mayumi: 
Oh my… Thank you very much! 
Kiara: 
You’re welcome.  That book is a souvenir for Mother. That 
hat is Father’s.  This T-shirt is Jun’s.  This candy is your 
older brother’s and your younger sister’s. 
Jun:  
Thank you very much! 
Kiara: 
Jun, how many people are in the class?  I have souvenirs for 
everyone. 
Jun:  
Forty people. 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B7:
第２課の4 
 
Jun:  
Here is Ochanomizu.  There are many bookstores (here). 
Store clerk: Welcome! 
Jun:  
Kiara, notebooks are here. 
Kiara:  
Umm, that pencil and two of those erasers please. 
 
 
And then, that notebook and that manga over there, please. 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B8:
第２課の5 
 
Kiara:  
 
Oh… I’m hungry (my stomach is rumbling). 
Jun:  
 
I’m hungry too.  Mom, what’s for dinner? 
Mayumi: 
 
(We’re having) tako! 
Kiara:  
 
Taco? 
Mayumi: 
 
Ai, Dad, dinner…! 
Aiko and Tarou: Yes! 
Mayumi: 
 
OK, here is taco.  Please (have some)! 
Kiara:  
 
This is taco? 
 
 

---PAGE_BREAK---
B9:
Beginning Japanese Dialog Translations 
 
 
 
第３課の１ 
 
Jun: The middle school is here. The high school is way over there. 
Kiara: Is there a college? 
Jun: Yes, it is my friend’s older brother’s college. 
Kiara: Is there an elementary school? 
Jun: No, there is no elementary school. 
Jun: Kiara, you are a 2nd-year high school student, aren’t you?  I’m a 1st-
year student.  Well, let’s go into the school. 
Kiara: Yes, let’s go in. 
Jun: Oh! No shoes! 
Kiara: I’m sorry.  Where is the classroom? 
Jun: (Room) 1-3 is way over there.  The teacher is also over there.  This is 
Teacher Yamamoto.  He is an English teacher. 
Kiara: How do you do?  I am Kiara. I am very pleased to meet you. 
(Teacher) Mr. Yamamoto: 
Oh, so you are Kiara?  Howdo you do? Pleased 
to meet you.  Please sit here. 

---PAGE_BREAK---

ABSOLUTE BEGINNER S2 #7 - HOW MUCH DO THINGS COST IN JAPAN?
4
いくら
いくら
ikura
how much (asking 
price)
SAMPLE SENTENCES
ダイヤモンドはたかいです。
Daiyamondo wa takai desu. 
Diamonds are expensive.
え？そうですか？
E? Sō desu ka? 
Huh? Is that so?
いま、なんじですか。
Ima, nan-ji desu ka. 
What time is it now?
トマトは７８えんです。
Tomato wa nana-jū hachi-en desu. 
A tomato is 78 yen.
オーストラリアドルはいくらですか？
Ōsutoraria doru wa ikura desu ka. 
How much is the Australian dollar?
ほんとうにすみません。
Hontō ni sumimasen. 
I'm really sorry.
これは１００えんです。
Kore wa 100-en desu. 
This is 100 yen.
１ドルです。
Ichi-doru desu. 
It's one dollar.
コーラはいくらですか。
Kōra wa ikura desu ka. 
How much is the cola?
VOCABULARY PHRASE USAGE
 えん (en)  
 En is the Japanese pronunciation of the Japanese currency, the yen. Unlike the English 
pronunciation, however, there's no [-y] sound, so be careful! 
Let's also check how to pronounce "dollar" and "Euro" in Japanese... 
1.
ドル (doru) → "dollar"
2.
ユーロ (yūro) → "euro"

C03:
第9 課の２ 
 
Jun:  
 
I am sorry! 
salesperson: 
Coming.  Welcome. 
Ben:  
 
How much is this book? 
salesperson: 
Yes, 1 volume is ￥1,025. 
Ben:  
 
Well, two volumes please. 
salesperson: 
Yes, two volumes, ￥2,050. 
Kiara: 
 
Excuse me, three brushes as well please. 
salesperson: 
Yes, one  is ￥1,500, so three are ￥4,500.  All 
for ￥6,550. 
Tomo: 
Well, take it from ￥10,000 please. 
salesperson: 
Yes, from ￥10,000. 
 
Well then,  your change is￥3,450.  Thank you 
very much.  Come again!
